name: Discount Calculator
description: Industrial-grade discount analysis tool for evaluating pricing strategies, margin impact, and volume trade-offs. Aligns with ISO 9001 cost management and Lean Six Sigma value stream principles.

inputs.label: List Price (per unit)
inputs.businessContext: Standard selling price before any discount, as defined in ISO 9001 cost accounting procedures.

inputs.label: Discount Percentage
inputs.businessContext: Negotiated or promotional discount off list price, typical in Lean pricing strategies.

inputs.label: Order Quantity
inputs.businessContext: Number of units in the transaction, used for volume-based discount analysis per WERC DC metrics.

inputs.label: Variable Cost per Unit
inputs.businessContext: Direct material, labor, and variable overhead per unit (ISO 14051 MFCA standard).

inputs.label: Fixed Cost Allocation (total)
inputs.businessContext: Allocated fixed costs for the order batch (e.g., setup, engineering, admin) per Lean cost deployment.

inputs.label: Discount Type
inputs.businessContext: Method of discount application: percentage off list or fixed dollar amount per unit.

inputs.label: Apply Discount to All Units
inputs.businessContext: If true, discount applies to entire quantity; if false, only to incremental units (tiered pricing).

rules.message: Discounted price is at or below variable cost. This may violate ISO 9001 profitability thresholds.
Order quantity is low; consider minimum order quantity policies per Lean lot sizing.
Fixed cost allocation exceeds 50% of total list revenue. Review cost allocation method (ABC).

thresholds.message: Net margin is negative. Immediate pricing review recommended per Six Sigma DMAIC.
Net margin below 10%. Consider cost reduction or price optimization (Lean value stream).
Discount exceeds 30%. Validate strategic intent and impact on brand value (ISO 20437).

formulas.label: Discount Amount per Unit
formulas.description: Calculated discount per unit based on type and list price.

formulas.label: Discounted Price per Unit
formulas.description: Net price after discount per unit.

formulas.label: Total Revenue
formulas.description: Total revenue from the order after discount.

formulas.label: Total Variable Cost
formulas.description: Total variable cost for the order quantity.

formulas.label: Total Cost
formulas.description: Sum of variable and fixed costs for the order.

formulas.label: Net Margin Percentage
formulas.description: Profit margin as a percentage of total revenue.

primary.label: Net Margin
primary.description: Primary financial outcome: profit after discount and all costs.

hiddenLossDrivers.description: Discounts exceeding the break-even threshold erode profit. Compare discount_amount_per_unit to (list_price - variable_cost_per_unit).
If fixed cost allocation is not fully absorbed by the discounted revenue, hidden loss occurs.
High discount may require disproportionate volume increase to maintain same net margin (Lean throughput accounting).

suggestedActions.description: If net_margin_percent < 10, consider reducing discount or negotiating lower variable costs with suppliers (Lean sourcing).
If fixed_cost_allocation > 0.3 * total_revenue, review fixed cost drivers using activity-based costing (ABC).
If discount_percent > 20, evaluate if the discount is tied to a strategic volume increase or market share gain (Six Sigma project charter).
If net_margin < 0, immediately halt discount and perform root cause analysis (DMAIC).

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted Net Margin
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: Net margin adjusted for data quality and input variability (Monte Carlo simulation readiness).
dataConfidenceAdjusted.methodology: Based on completeness of input fields, historical accuracy of cost data, and adherence to ISO 9001 data governance.
